dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c Markram b Mohammed Shami 7 (8b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 87.5

dismissalComment.html: great catching!! LSG's fielding and Shami's skills have combined for the second wicket of the day. Head is beaten by the lack of pace as well - like Abhishek was. Shami bowls it on a short of a length on off, as he angles it across, and bowls with a scrambled seam at just 121kph. Head looks to push forward, but ends up losing the bottom hand off his bat, and awkwardly pops it up for Markram at mid-off. Markram does really well to dive forward and grab it low

dismissalText.commentary: Abhishek Sharma c Siddharth b Mohammed Shami 0 (2b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: gone!! The slip was just removed, but the man at short third completes a nice take. Siddharth placed there went low and dived to his right to grab the ball just above the ground. It was an outside edge off Abhishek's bat, as he went driving at this full delivery outside off. It was a slower ball, though - a really early use of the variation - from Shami, and Abhishek goes for a duck. Just one run from the first over, apart from the wicket

dismissalText.commentary: Ishan Kishan b Prince Yadav 1 (4b 0x4 0x6 13m) SR: 25

dismissalComment.html: bowled him!! Prince celebrates like a royal. He has flattened Kishan's off stump. Prince angled it across, and went with an upright seam. The ball landed around off or thereabouts, and Kishan went driving. But the ball just about shaped back in enough to smack the off stump, as Kishan was beaten

dismissalText.commentary: Liam Livingstone c †Pant b Rathi 14 (20b 0x4 1x6 24m) SR: 70

dismissalComment.html: superb take by Pant!! He first leaped in excitement, then moved to his left with the angle, and finally dived across to his right to gobble it one-handed. This is what happened with the ball: Livingstone went for the lap sweep, but ended up top-edging it. The ball popped up behind him, and Pant was alert to the opportunity, although his jump gave the impression that he thought Livingstone was hit on the pad. In fact, replays show the ball went up off Livingstone's left shoulder

dismissalText.commentary: Abhishek Sharma c Siddharth b Mohammed Shami 0 (2b 0x4 0x6 4m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: gone!! The slip was just removed, but the man at short third completes a nice take. Siddharth placed there went low and dived to his right to grab the ball just above the ground. It was an outside edge off Abhishek's bat, as he went driving at this full delivery outside off. It was a slower ball, though - a really early use of the variation - from Shami, and Abhishek goes for a duck. Just one run from the first over, apart from the wicket

dismissalText.commentary: Travis Head c Markram b Mohammed Shami 7 (8b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 87.5

dismissalComment.html: great catching!! LSG's fielding and Shami's skills have combined for the second wicket of the day. Head is beaten by the lack of pace as well - like Abhishek was. Shami bowls it on a short of a length on off, as he angles it across, and bowls with a scrambled seam at just 121kph. Head looks to push forward, but ends up losing the bottom hand off his bat, and awkwardly pops it up for Markram at mid-off. Markram does really well to dive forward and grab it low

dismissalText.commentary: Liam Livingstone c †Pant b Rathi 14 (20b 0x4 1x6 24m) SR: 70

dismissalComment.html: superb take by Pant!! He first leaped in excitement, then moved to his left with the angle, and finally dived across to his right to gobble it one-handed. This is what happened with the ball: Livingstone went for the lap sweep, but ended up top-edging it. The ball popped up behind him, and Pant was alert to the opportunity, although his jump gave the impression that he thought Livingstone was hit on the pad. In fact, replays show the ball went up off Livingstone's left shoulder

dismissalText.commentary: Nicholas Pooran run out (†Ishan Kishan) 1 (4b 0x4 0x6 3m) SR: 25

dismissalComment.html: Pooran finds a new way to lose his wicket... It's a touch unfortunate. He swept it into the pitch, Kishan moved right and caught it on the bounce, Pooran didn't know that and took a couple of steps. When he turned, the bails were off. The ball spun away a touch which might be why the ball went that fine. Any which way, rare dismissal
